frameworks.name: fMRI-Decoded Neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: real-time fMRI feedback induces threat-related neural patterns without conscious cue awareness

frameworks.name: exposure-based therapy
frameworks.one_line: conventional therapy that requires conscious confrontation with feared cues

frameworks.name: nonconscious threat processing
frameworks.one_line: trauma-related neural activity decoded and modulated below awareness threshold

core_question: Can fMRI-decoded neurofeedback reduce PTSD symptoms by inducing threat-related neural patterns without conscious exposure?
key_assumptions: neural activation patterns for feared cues can be decoded and re-induced
therapeutic effect does not require conscious awareness of trauma cues
DecNef is compatible with within-subject crossover designs
manipulations: active DecNef vs placebo neurofeedback assignment
crossover order across a six-month interval
improved decoding procedure for nonconscious trauma-cue activity
limitations_noted: prior DecNef evidence limited to laboratory settings and small patient samples
